commit.message:
fix(backend): robust URL parser — split on last @ to handle password with @

pg-connection-string uses Node's url.parse which splits on the FIRST @,
mangling passwords that contain @ (encoded or raw).

Replace connectionString Pool config with a manual parser that:
- Finds the LAST @ as the user/host boundary
- Calls decodeURIComponent on the password (handles both raw @ and %40)
- Passes individual params to pg.Pool, bypassing url.parse entirely

Works with both Render dashboard URL (raw @) and .env URL (%40).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: SKU search dropdown now matches single letters like 'D'

Choices.js v10 uses Fuse.js for search which defaults to location-biased
matching (position 0). All SKU labels start with 'Standard_', pushing the
series letter to position 9, causing zero results for single-char searches.

Fix: add fuseOptions to the Choices.js init:
  - ignoreLocation: true  -> search entire string, not just the start
  - minMatchCharLength: 1 -> enable single-character searches
  - threshold: 0.3        -> tighter match quality
  - searchResultLimit: 200 -> show more results (default was 4)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Defer PDF persistence until activity creation; fix filename collisions and download naming (#17)

* Initial plan

* Defer PDF persistence until activity creation, add cache with cleanup, fix naming

- PDFService: cachePdf() stores uploads in-memory instead of persisting immediately
- PDFService: finalizePdf() persists to filesystem + DB when activity is created
- PDFService: getPdfContent/getPdfDocument check cache first, then DB
- PDFService: updatePdfExtractionResults updates cache when PDF not yet finalized
- PDFService: Scheduled cache cleanup every 5min (TTL=60min, max 100 entries)
- PDFService: UUID-prefixed filenames prevent same-name upload collisions
- PDFDocument: Removed @GeneratedValue to allow manual ID from cache key
- ActivityService: createActivityWithValidation calls finalizePdf before creating
- ActivityService: uploadAndCreateActivity uses cache→finalize flow
- ActivityController: getActivityPdf uses activity name as download filename
- DocumentsController: upload endpoint uses cachePdf instead of storePdf
- LearnHubApplication: Added @EnableScheduling for cache cleanup
- Added 10 unit tests for cache operations, finalization, naming, and eviction

Co-authored-by: milljoniaer <78978542+milljoniaer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: sanitize activity name in download filename, rename evict method

The cache key is now purely temporary. finalizePdf() lets JPA auto-generate the
document ID and returns it. createActivityWithValidation() and
uploadAndCreateActivity() use the returned ID as the activity's document_id.

commit.message:
fix: smart flex distribution for ResizablePanel sizing

Previous fix allowed shrinking but panels had flex-grow: 0, so they never
filled available space — causing 100px-wide content areas.

New strategy uses two modes:
- With a flex panel: non-flex siblings stay fixed at defaultSize via
  flex-basis, flex panel fills remaining space (existing behavior)
- Without any flex panel: distribute space proportionally by defaultSize
  ratio (e.g. 15/60/25 → 15%/60%/25% of container)

After drag resize, the effect pins the panel at the new size via
flex-basis, and sibling panels adjust naturally.

interpolation cannot be translated to SQL — EF Core 3+ throws an

causing the API to return 500 and the frontend to fall back to demo
data. Fix: fetch a plain anonymous-type projection from the DB (all
SQL-safe), then map to EventFeedItemDto in memory where local
computations (CDN URL, hot IDs, dropping-soon) are evaluated.

commit.message:
fix(shared): sanitize logger context and harden PII sanitizer (M-17–M-23)

M-17: Logger now runs all context objects through sanitizePII before
logging to console or transport, preventing credentials/secrets leaks.

M-18: ErrorHandlingService config objects are automatically sanitized
via the logger's new sanitizeContext path — no separate fix needed.

M-19: Replace `as Error & {...}` type assertion in createError with
Object.assign for proper type inference without casting.

M-20: Remove bare 'key' from SENSITIVE_KEYS to avoid false-positive
redaction of legitimate fields (e.g. primaryKey, keyName).

M-21: Add missing sensitive key patterns: ssn, social_security,
credit_card, cvc, pin, credentials, access_key.

M-22: Remove all 3+1 `as` casts in pii-sanitizer (as object x2,
as Record, JSON.parse as unknown) using type narrowing and annotations.

M-23: Add .trim() before JSON-in-string detection so leading/trailing
whitespace no longer bypasses embedded JSON sanitization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit RHI barrier before GPU readback to prevent tearing

On Vulkan, vkCmdCopyImageToBuffer can race with fragment shader writes
when no explicit pipeline barrier separates the render target write and
the copy.  Metal handles this implicitly via resource tracking, which is
why macOS had no tearing.

Insert RHICmdList.Transition(RTV → CopySrc) before EnqueueCopy and
transition back (CopySrc → RTV) afterward so the next CaptureScene can
write.  This ensures all render target writes are flushed and visible
before the DMA begins, eliminating bottom-half tearing on Linux/Vulkan.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestion from @Copilot

commit.message:
fix: prevent non-flex ResizablePanel overflow on constrained viewports

Non-flex panels used flex: none (flex-shrink: 0), preventing them from
shrinking below content width. Wide content pushed layout past viewport.

- Wrapper: flex: none → flex: 0 1 auto (allow shrinking to minSize)
- Wrapper: apply defaultSize as initial CSS width/height
- Effect: stop overriding flex to none after drag resize
- Inner panel: remove flexShrink: 0 for non-flex panels

Panels now shrink gracefully when the container is too narrow, respecting
minSize as the lower bound. Fixes overflow across all ResizablePanelGroup
consumers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
